parts.text: Hi, I have a wheat field at latitude 19.5 and longitude 75.3, field size is 2.5ha, planted on 2026-05-10.

Hi there! Based on weather forecasts and crop needs, you only need to irrigate on two days this week. On June 27, apply 122,038 liters, and on June 28, apply a light 8,550 liters. From June 29 to July 3, you can completely turn off your pumps because rainfall will fully cover your crop's water needs. By factoring in this rain, you save a massive 919,500 liters of water—an outstanding 87.6% reduction compared to watering blindly!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.

Hi there! Based on weather forecasts and crop needs, you only need to irrigate on two days this week. On June 27, apply 122,038 liters, and on June 28, apply a light 8,550 liters. From June 29 to July 3, you can completely turn off your pumps because rainfall will fully cover your crop's water needs. By factoring in this rain, you save a massive 919,500 liters of water—an outstanding 87.6% reduction compared to watering blindly!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.

For the upcoming week, you only need to irrigate on two days: apply 240,202 liters on June 27th and 17,303 liters on June 28th. From June 29th through July 3rd, you can turn off the irrigation entirely because the forecasted rain will fully meet your crops' needs. By taking advantage of this rainfall, you will save a massive 1,842,500 liters of water—reducing your usage by 87.7% compared to a standard baseline schedule!

Advisory: This recommendation supplements, does not replace, local agricultural extension advice.
